tokenizer_exposure.detail: Admitted source appears in tokenizer training and model training, but benchmark publication requires a disjoint held-out path.
Normative source remains available to tokenizer and training, with benchmark use allowed only under section-disjointness rules.
Tokenizer-only exposure is recorded explicitly and remains distinct from any model-training exposure.
Held-out evaluation source remains benchmark-visible but excluded from tokenizer and model-training loaders.
Rejected source remains excluded from every loader surface.

section_range_rules.rationale: Books must split on stable chapter and section anchors so train and benchmark prose never share the same authored section.
Specifications may only reuse the same source across train and benchmark when section anchors remain disjoint.
Manuals require page-range isolation because scans and OCR slices are often page-addressed rather than chapter-addressed.
Papers must isolate train and benchmark material by page ranges or equivalent stable section anchors.
Short technical docs stay whole-source disjoint unless a later contract defines more granular stable record anchors.
